skill.name: visioncraft
skill.description: Generate images and videos via Agnes AI. Triggers on any request to create, draw, render, design, edit, restyle, or animate visual content — pictures, posters, illustrations, product visuals, photos, or video clips. Handles text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, multi-image video, and keyframe animation.
skill.summary: Generate images and videos through the Agnes AI API using PowerShell scripts, supporting text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, and keyframe animation modes.
skill.author: BAI-32

security_audit.summary: VisionCraft is a legitimate creative tool that calls the Agnes AI API via PowerShell scripts. The static analyzer produced an overwhelming number of false positives because it flagged backtick syntax and inline code in markdown documentation (.md files) as shell execution patterns, and treated API key setup instructions as credential exposure. The actual scripts (gen_image.ps1, gen_video.ps1) are straightforward: they read the AGNES_API_KEY environment variable, POST to a single hardcoded endpoint (https://apihub.agnes-ai.com), and return generated media URLs. No data exfiltration, no obfuscation, no command injection vectors. Confidence in safety is high.

low_findings.title: PowerShell scripts require API key and make external API calls
low_findings.description: The gen_image.ps1 and gen_video.ps1 scripts read AGNES_API_KEY from environment and POST to https://apihub.agnes-ai.com. This is expected functionality for the skill's stated purpose of generating images/videos via Agnes AI. Users must supply their own API key. No exfiltration or misuse patterns detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Read the actual scripts - they use Invoke-RestMethod/Invoke-WebRequest with hardcoded HTTPS URLs to the documented Agnes AI API. Behavior matches the skill's stated purpose.

content.user_title: Generate AI images and videos with simple commands
content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated images and videos usually requires navigating web interfaces or writing complex API code. VisionCraft lets you generate, edit, and animate visuals directly from Claude with simple natural-language prompts.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts with configurable resolution and aspect ratios
Transform existing images using image-to-image mode with a reference URL
Generate short video clips from text descriptions with configurable duration and resolution
Animate still images into video clips using image-to-video mode
Create multi-image videos blending several reference images
Produce smooth keyframe animations transitioning between start and end images
limitations: Requires a valid Agnes AI API key set as the AGNES_API_KEY environment variable
Video generation is limited to 441 frames maximum (roughly 7 seconds at 60fps)
Frame count must follow the 8n+1 rule (e.g., 81, 121, 161, 241, 441)
Depends on Agnes AI service availability — subject to their rate limits and uptime

use_cases.title: Content creators making social media visuals
use_cases.description: Quickly generate eye-catching images and short video clips for posts, stories, and marketing without leaving the conversation.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Designers prototyping visual concepts
use_cases.description: Sketch ideas, restyle references, and animate mockups to share with clients or iterate on creative direction.

use_cases.title: Developers integrating AI media generation
use_cases.description: Use the PowerShell scripts as a reference implementation for calling the Agnes AI image and video APIs from automation pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a basic image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a futuristic city skyline at sunset with neon lights reflected in the water.

prompt_templates.title: Transform an existing image
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the image at https://example.com/photo.png and transform it into a cyberpunk night scene, preserving the original composition.

prompt_templates.title: Create a short video clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6-second video of a cat walking along a beach at golden hour with gentle waves.

prompt_templates.title: Animate between two keyframes
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate a smooth transition from https://example.com/dawn.png to https://example.com/night.png, showing the sky darkening and lights turning on across a cityscape.
prompt_templates.scenario: Keyframe animation with multiple reference images

output_examples.input: User asks: Make a poster for a coffee shop called Morning Brew with a warm, rustic feel.
User asks: Animate this landscape photo into a 4-second video with gentle wind movement.
User asks: Generate a product photo of a sneaker on a white background.

best_practices: Set the AGNES_API_KEY once as a persistent user environment variable so it survives terminal restarts.
Use the -Ratio parameter (e.g., 16:9, 9:16, 1:1) instead of manually specifying width and height for common aspect ratios.
Echo the returned MEDIA: URL on its own line so chat platforms can render the image or video inline.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode your API key directly in scripts or commit it to version control — always use the environment variable.
Do not bury the generated media URL inside a paragraph of text — always place it on its own line starting with MEDIA:.
Do not download the generated media to local disk unless the user explicitly requests it.

faq.question: What API does VisionCraft use?
faq.answer: VisionCraft calls the Agnes AI API, specifically the agnes-image-2.1-flash model for images and agnes-video-v2.0 for videos.

faq.question: How do I set up the API key?
faq.answer: Set the AGNES_API_KEY environment variable in PowerShell. For a persistent setting, use [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable with the User scope. For the current session only, use $env:AGNES_API_KEY.

faq.question: What image and video sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Images default to 2048x2048 and support aspect ratios including 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 3:2, 2:3, and 21:9. Videos default to 1920x1080 at 60fps with 241 frames (~4 seconds).

faq.question: Can I animate a single still image into a video?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the image-to-video mode by passing a single image URL with the -Image parameter to gen_video.ps1 along with a motion description prompt.

faq.question: What happens if video generation takes too long?
faq.answer: The script polls for up to 15 minutes. If the video is not ready, it prints the video_id so you can manually re-query the API later without losing the task.

faq.question: Does VisionCraft store my images or videos?
faq.answer: No. VisionCraft only forwards your prompt and parameters to the Agnes AI API and returns the resulting media URL. No data is stored locally by the skill.
